accepted_evidence: The near representation now uses 388 branch-attached anchors, six 30-49 cm shoots per anchor, modeled tertiary branches and petioles, and 32592 padding-aware leaf cards whose visible leaves remain within the recorded 2-3.5 cm botanical range.
The far representation adds 240 deterministic interior crown anchors and retains 90432 masked twig-cluster cards, producing continuous nonuniform crown mass in both 60 m and 150 m views.

An isolated UE 5.8 Metal comparison rejects Nanite PreserveArea for this inexpensive procedural trunk because tapered ends facet in closeup; the selected non-Nanite path retains the full 1624-vertex structural mesh.

The leaf material now uses a valid scalar alpha output, a 0.50 masked cutoff, and TwoSidedSign-corrected tangent normals; the final Metal run has no material compile fallback and removes the earlier silver backface field.
The isolated map passes map check with zero errors and warnings and captures all five required views without corridor substitution.
rejection_reasons: The closeup exposes crowded branch origins, intersecting woody segments, abrupt taper joins, and leaf clusters that do not yet form clean source-matched ramification.
The denser silhouette is a clear improvement, but repeated horizontal shelves remain legible through the crown and prevent acceptance as a mature irregular coigue.
Bark and interior foliage remain too dark under the fixed review photometry, reducing close-range fissure and shoot readability.
Only one open-grown adult form exists; forest-grown adult, intermediate, juvenile, damaged, and deadwood variants remain absent.
No corridor ecology placement, temporal wind and LOD capture, guide or ecology review, or measured desktop and VR performance evidence exists.

views: outer-shoot bark, branchlet, petiole, and leaf closeup
review_contract.capture_redistribution: not approved; hashes and reproducible paths only are retained in the open-source repository

next_iteration_requirements: Replace overlapping radial shoot bundles with parent-child ramification whose origins, taper, and phyllotaxis remain readable in the outer-shoot closeup.
Author open-grown adult, forest-grown adult, and intermediate crown forms with distinct branch-angle, live-crown-ratio, asymmetry, and damage parameters.
Rebalance bark and foliage photometry without adding emissive fill or hiding material response.
Repeat all five views and add temporal wind plus 28-36 m LOD handoff evidence before any corridor placement.
